channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: The incidence of psychotic disorders has increased in more recent birth cohorts
item.description: 1. The incidence of psychotic disorders in Ontario has increased in more recent birth cohorts, and the age of diagnosis has decreased. Evidence Rating...

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: Potential Noninvasive Alternative to Right Heart Cath in HF
item.description: T2 mapping can match right heart catheterization for measuring the severity of heart failure. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Alzheimer Antibody Therapy Denied Approval in Scotland
item.description: The Scottish Medicines Consortium has rejected donanemab for early Alzheimer’s disease, citing inadequate clinical and economic evidence. Medscape New...

item.title: First-In-Class Neuroprotective Drug Improves Stroke Outcomes
item.description: A neuroprotective agent improved 90-day functional outcomes and reduced poststroke depression and anxiety in patients with ischemic stroke. Medscape M...

item.title: Fever chills explained: How brain signals push warmth-seeking to fight infection
item.description: When running a fever during infection, we often feel chills, which prompt us to take action to warm ourselves, such as turning on a heater or adding l...

item.title: Men resort to buying underground testosterone to bridge access gap
item.description: Some men are resorting to obtaining testosterone replacement therapy (TRT) from underground sources due to significant barriers to accessing the thera...

item.title: AI stethoscope can help spot 'silent epidemic' of heart valve disease earlier than GPs, study suggests
item.description: Artificial intelligence could help doctors detect serious heart valve disease years earlier, potentially saving thousands of lives, a new study sugges...

item.title: Long COVID linked to Alzheimer's disease mechanisms
item.description: The increased size of, and lesser blood supply to, a key brain structure in patients with long COVID tracks with known blood markers of Alzheimer's di...

item.title: Sublingual Therapy Effective in Seasonal Asthma and Rhinitis
item.description: Sublingual immunotherapy for grass and dust mites reduces the proportions of patients experiencing asthma exacerbations and frequently using reliever ...

item.title: Controlled 'oxidative spark' may serve as a surprising ally in brain repair
item.description: Oxidative stress is a direct consequence of an excess in the body of so-called free radicals—reactive, unstable molecules that contain oxygen. Free ra...

item.title: Rehab Therapy Beneficial in Children With Perinatal Stroke
item.description: A high-dose rehabilitation therapy improves motor outcomes in infants with perinatal stroke, offering rare evidence to guide early intervention during...

item.title: 2 Minute Medicine Rewind February 9, 2026
item.description: Reproductive Shifts and Ovarian Cancer Risk in Women Aged 40 Years or Older 1. Across the premenopausal and postmenopausal groups, early menarche, lat...

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Modest 'mental exercise' can reduce risk of dementia for decades, study finds
item.description: One type of cognitive training appears to reduce the risk of dementia 20 years later.

item.title: Beyond weight loss: the expanding benefits of GLP-1 receptor agonists
item.description: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1 RAs) were introduced to help manage type 2 diabetes. They improve glycemic control and promote weight...

item.title: AI-assisted therapy: Why supervision makes the difference
item.description: Artificial intelligence in mental health has become an easy target. Headlines warn of hallucinating chatbots, reckless advice, and sensational claims ...

item.title: When language becomes the barrier: IMGs and autism diagnoses
item.description: I once left grand rounds with a donut and a fake pager buzz because I couldn’t understand the speaker, who often was a natural Mandarin or Arabic spea...

item.title: Simple choices prevent chronic disease [PODCAST]

item.title: Medical misinformation: Navigating vaccine hesitancy with empathy
item.description: Medical misinformation is rampant but should not be overstated. Vaccination remains a good barometer of the range of opinions on a subject directly re...

item.title: An ultrasound test may more reliably detect ovarian cancer in premenopausal women than the Risk of Malignancy Index
item.description: 1. This cohort study found that the IOTA ADNEX ultrasound model at a 10% risk threshold detected more ovarian cancers in premenopausal women than the ...

item.title: Chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy may induce sustained remission in multirefractory autoimmune hemolytic anemia
item.description: 1. In this prospective cohort study, CD19-directed chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy was found to elicit a complete response in eleven pa...

item.title: Neonatal Hyperbilirubinemia and navigating the 2022 AAP guideline updates
item.description: Key Points: The 2022 American Academy of Pediatrics guidelines slightly raised phototherapy thresholds for most infants to balance preventing encephal...
